concerns.text: Task T013-VAL is marked '[US1] Sequential Dependency' but lacks a [P] tag, yet it is listed alongside T013-A and T013-B which are marked [P]. T013-VAL depends on the *output* of T013-A/B (downloaded files) to trigger the validator. If T013-A/B run in parallel with T013-VAL, T013-VAL will fail or race. T013-VAL must be explicitly ordered AFTER T013-A/B, and T013-A/B should not be marked [P] relative to T013-VAL if they share the same execution context. The current grouping implies T013-VAL can run in parallel with the downloads it validates, which is a logical impossibility.

Task T022 (likelihood with banded covariance) depends on T015 (full covariance construction) and T015-TRANS (banded approximation generation). T022 is marked [US2] but has no explicit dependency on T015/T015-TRANS. While US2 generally depends on US1, the specific artifact 'banded covariance' is produced by T015-TRANS. If T022 runs before T015-TRANS completes, it will fail. The task list must explicitly reflect that T022 cannot start until T015-TRANS is complete, or T015-TRANS must be moved to Phase 2 (Foundational) if it is a strict prerequisite for all inference.

Task T016 (fallback logic for bootstrap) depends on the outcome of T013-A/B (checking if < 3 runs are found). T016 is listed as a separate task but implies a conditional execution path based on T013-A/B results. If T016 is a distinct implementation task, it must be ordered after T013-A/B. If it is part of T013-A/B logic, it should be merged. Currently, the ordering suggests T016 could be implemented independently of the download logic, which breaks the data-flow dependency.

Task T013-A and T013-B are both marked [P] (parallel). However, they both write to the same logical artifact (the raw data directory) and T013-VAL validates the *combined* result. If they run truly in parallel, race conditions on file locking or validation state could occur. The [P] tag is risky here; they should be sequential or the validation task must be explicitly decoupled to run after both complete.
